info.title: Unified Approvals Connector
info.description:
The Unified Approvals Connector is the HTTP API for managing approvals on Workfront document versions. Use it to create approvals, build multi-stage workflows with conditional stage triggers, add and remind participants, record decisions, manage reusable approval templates, search across every approval the requesting user can see, and retrieve AI brand-review results captured by bot reviewers on a stage.

## Authentication

The connector supports one authentication method:

| Method | Header/Cookie | Use case |
|--------|--------------|----------|
| **Bearer** | `Authorization: Bearer <token>` | IMS Bearer token from Adobe IO / Developer Console |

All endpoints accept Bearer token auth.

> **Note on auth error codes:** The AIO gateway performs credential pre-flight before routing to the service. If the `Authorization` header is absent entirely, the gateway returns HTTP 403 (`error_code: 403010`, "Oauth token is missing.") and the request never reaches the service. If a token is present but invalid or expired, the gateway forwards the request and the service returns HTTP 401 (`error_code: 401013`).

## Asset Types

Endpoints currently only support `DOCV` (Workfront document version) as the `assetType` path parameter.


## Base URL

`https://workfront.adobe.io/workfront-approvals/`

## Endpoint Groups

| Tag | Base path | Auth |
|-----|-----------|------|
| Approvals | `/api/v1/approvals` | Complex |
| Templates | `/api/v1/templates` | Complex |
| Bots | `/api/v1/bots` | Complex |
| Bot Reviews | `/api/v1/bot-reviews` | Complex |

tags.name: Decisions
tags.description: Make and delete decisions on approval stages

tags.name: Bots
tags.description: Retrieve Workfront bot users that are eligible to be added as reviewer participants on an approval. A bot reviewer is invoked automatically when its assigned stage becomes active.

tags.name: Bot Reviews
tags.description: AI brand review results from the Bot Registry

Bearer.description: IMS Bearer token from Adobe IO / Developer Console.

StageId.name: stageId
StageId.description: The ID of the approval stage

TemplateId.name: templateId
TemplateId.description: The ID of the approval template

XRequestId.name: x-request-id
XRequestId.description: Unique identifier for request tracing. Auto-generated if not provided.

XCorrelationId.name: x-correlation-id
XCorrelationId.description: Unique identifier for correlation across multiple service calls. Auto-generated if not provided.

XApiKey.name: x-api-key
XApiKey.description: `client_id` of the Adobe Developer Console technical account. Identifies the calling integration to the AIO gateway.

WfUserId.name: wf-userid
WfUserId.description: Workfront user ID of the acting user.

Approval.description: An approval record for an asset

realCreatorId.description: ID of the proxy user who actually created the approval. When the approval is created on behalf of another user (e.g. via a technical account or another proxying integration), this is the ID of the actual user making the call (the technical-account / proxy user). The companion `creatorId` field still reflects the user the action is attributed to. When no proxying takes place, this matches the companion field.

templateId.description: ID of the template used to create this approval, if any.

productId.description: Identifier of the Workfront product the approval is associated with. Always `workfront` for the Unified Approvals Connector.

status.description:
Overall lifecycle status of the approval, derived from the rolled-up stage and participant decisions.

- `in review` — At least one stage is active and waiting for decisions.
- `pending review` — Approval exists but no stage has been activated yet.
- `approved` — All required participants have approved.
- `approved with changes` — All required participants have decided; at least one with changes.
- `needs work` — At least one required participant has returned needs work.
- `reviewed` — All reviewer-role participants have reviewed (no approver decisions required).
- `withdrawn` — The approval has been retracted; no further actions are possible.

isLocked.description: Whether the approval is currently locked. Mirrors the lock state of the active stage (a stage being manually locked sets the approval inactive).

isApprovalActive.description: Computed flag that indicates whether the version being accessed is still the document's active (head) version. Compares the document's `currentVersionID` against the `assetId` of the requested version: when they match, the version is current and `isApprovalActive` is `true`; when the document has been superseded by a newer upload, it is `false`.

isEsmAsset.description: Whether the asset is an ESM (Experience Storage Manager) asset

frameSyncStatus.description: Whether the Frame.io asset sync is complete

userInfo.description: Contextual information about the authenticated user's relationship to this approval

isEditable.description: Whether the requesting user can make changes to this approval. Computed per request as `canEdit AND isActive AND hasLicenseAccess` — the user must have edit permission on the underlying asset, the approval must be in an active state, and the user must have a Workfront license tier that grants edit access. Used by write-path middleware to gate mutating requests and surfaced on every approval response so consumers can mirror the same gate in the UI.

hasLicenseAccess.description: Whether the requesting user's Workfront license tier grants the access needed to edit this approval. One of the inputs to `isEditable`.

activeDecision.description: The current user's most recent decision

action.description:
The next decision-related action the requesting user can take on this approval, derived from the user's `participantRole` and current `activeDecision`. Frontends typically render this as a button label.

| Role / Decision | `userInfo.action` |
|---|---|
| `approver` (no decision yet) | `approve` |
| `reviewer` (no decision yet) | `review` |
| Decision = `approved` | `revoke` |
| Decision = `needs work` | `approve` |
| Decision = `reviewed` | `review` |

availableDecisions.description: Map of decision key to display label for the current user

paths.description: Parallel paths in this approval. Present only when the approval was created with `paths`. Each path contains its own stages and structure.

stageId.description: Identifier for this stage. Must be unique across all stages on the approval. Can be a short, human-readable slug (e.g. `design-review`).

status.description:
Lifecycle status of the stage.

- `not started` — Stage has not yet been triggered to `isActive`. Set on creation and remains until the stage's activation trigger fires (typically when its parent stages complete).
- `pending review` — Stage is active and awaiting participant decisions.
- `in review` — Stage is active and at least one participant has not yet decided.
- `approved` — All required participants on this stage have approved.
- `approved with changes` — All required participants have decided, at least one has chosen `approved with changes`, and the rest have chosen `approved` or `approved with changes`. If any required participant has returned `needs work`, the stage rolls up to `needs work` instead.
- `needs work` — At least one required participant has responded with `needs work`. `needs work` outranks `approved` and `approved with changes`, so the stage rolls up to this status whenever any required participant has returned `needs work`.
- `reviewed` — All reviewer participants have reviewed.

deadlineTime.description: Deadline time of day in 24-hour `HH:MM` format (e.g. `"17:00"`).

deadlineTimeOfDay.description:
Time-of-day component of the stage deadline. An object with two numeric properties — `hours` (0–23) and `minutes` (0–59) — that, paired with the date derived from `deadlineBusinessDays`, pins the deadline to a specific moment. Set to `null` when no time-of-day is configured.

Example: `{ "hours": 17, "minutes": 0 }` for 5:00 PM.

isLocked.description: Stage has been manually locked (set inactive).

isLockOverridden.description: Stage lock was overridden by an admin.

isStarted.description: Stage has been started at least once.

isCompleted.description: Stage has reached a terminal state.

stageMetadata.description: Almost entirely a Frame.io integration artifact — when a Frame.io share is created for the stage it holds the generated review link and comments link. Defaults to `{}` if no Frame share was created (for example, on non-ESM or non-Frame assets).

parentIds.description: IDs of stages that must complete before this stage activates.

customMessage.description: Optional message sent to participants when this stage is activated

pathId.description: ID of the path this stage belongs to. Present when the approval uses paths.

participantRole.description:
Role this participant plays on the stage.

- `approver` — Has a binding vote; their decision directly affects stage and approval status.
- `reviewer` — Non-binding; can mark reviewed but does not block approval progression.

The role, combined with the participant's current decision, also drives the `userInfo.action` value on every approval response — see [`Approval.userInfo.action`](#section/Schemas/Approval) for the role/decision → action mapping.

requesterId.description: ID of the user who added this participant

realRequesterId.description: ID of the proxy user who actually added this participant to the stage. When the participant is added on behalf of another user (e.g. via a technical account or another proxying integration), this is the ID of the actual user making the call (the technical-account / proxy user). The companion `requesterId` field still reflects the user the action is attributed to. When no proxying takes place, this matches the companion field.

status.description:
Lifecycle status of this participant on the stage.

- `notified` — Participant has been sent a notification but has not yet opened or decided.
- `opened` — Participant has opened the review but has not yet decided.
- `commented` — Participant has left a comment but has not made a final decision.
- `approved` — Participant has approved.
- `approved with changes` — Participant has approved with changes requested.
- `needs work` — Participant returned needs work.
- `reviewed` — Participant has marked the review complete (reviewer role only).
- `not started` — Participant is on a stage that has not yet become active; no notification has been sent.

realUserId.description: ID of the proxy user who actually performed the activity. When the activity is performed on behalf of another user (e.g. via a technical account or another proxying integration), this is the ID of the actual user making the call (the technical-account / proxy user). The companion `userId` field still reflects the user the action is attributed to. When no proxying takes place, this matches the companion field.

autoLockEnabled.description: Automatically lock the stage when all participants have decided

decisionRules.description: Override rule for when the stage is considered decided. `onlyOneDecisionRequired` marks the stage complete when any one participant decides.

StageTrigger.description: Defines when and how a stage is automatically activated

when.description: `onCreate` activates the stage immediately when the approval is created. `onStagesComplete` activates after specified parent stages complete.

stages.description: Stage IDs that must complete to fire this trigger (used with `onStagesComplete`)

allowedDecisions.description: Decisions that count as 'complete' for this trigger

activateOnDecision.description: Number of qualifying decisions on the parent stages that must be reached before the stage activates. Combined with `allowedDecisions` to define which decisions count toward the threshold. Defaults to all required decisions on the listed stages.

ApprovalStructure.description: Represents one node in the stage dependency graph

stageId.description: Identifier of the stage this structure node represents. Matches the `stageId` on one of the approval's `stages[]` entries; must be unique across all stages on the approval (e.g. `design-review`).

parentIds.description: IDs of stages that must complete before this stage activates.

pathId.description: ID of the path this structure node belongs to. Present when the approval uses paths.

StageBody.description: Stage definition used when creating or updating an approval

deadlineTime.description: Deadline time of day in 24-hour `HH:MM` format (e.g. `"17:00"`).

stageMetadata.description: Almost entirely a Frame.io integration artifact — when a Frame.io share is created for the stage it holds the generated review link and comments link. Defaults to `{}` if no Frame share was created (for example, on non-ESM or non-Frame assets).

participantRole.description:
Role this participant plays on the stage.

- `approver` — Has a binding vote; their decision directly affects stage and approval status.
- `reviewer` — Non-binding; can mark reviewed but does not block approval progression.

The role, combined with the participant's current decision, also drives the `userInfo.action` value on every approval response — see [`Approval.userInfo.action`](#section/Schemas/Approval) for the role/decision → action mapping.

parentIds.description: IDs of stages that must complete before this stage activates.

customMessage.description: Optional message for participants when this stage activates

pathId.description: Path this stage belongs to. Only relevant when creating or updating with `paths`.

ParticipantBody.description: Participant definition used in request bodies

stageId.description: ID of an existing stage on the approval to attach this participant to (e.g. `design-review`).

participantRole.description:
Role this participant plays on the stage.

- `approver` — Has a binding vote; their decision directly affects stage and approval status.
- `reviewer` — Non-binding; can mark reviewed but does not block approval progression.

The role, combined with the participant's current decision, also drives the `userInfo.action` value on every approval response — see [`Approval.userInfo.action`](#section/Schemas/Approval) for the role/decision → action mapping.

timeframeEstimate.description: Computed timeframe estimate, in business days, derived from the sum of each stage's `deadlineBusinessDays` value. Read-only.

structure.description: Workflow structure defining stage ordering and trigger dependencies.

stageId.description: Identifier for this template stage. Must be unique across all stages within the template (e.g. `design-review`).

deadlineTimeOfDay.description:
Time-of-day component of the stage deadline. An object with two numeric properties — `hours` (0–23) and `minutes` (0–59) — that, paired with the date derived from `deadlineBusinessDays`, pins the deadline to a specific moment. Set to `null` when no time-of-day is configured.

Example: `{ "hours": 17, "minutes": 0 }` for 5:00 PM.

participantType.description:
Type of participant.

- `USER` — Individual Workfront user (or AI bot user).
- `TEAMOB` — Workfront team.

participantRole.description:
Role this participant plays on the stage.

- `approver` — Has a binding vote; their decision directly affects stage and approval status.
- `reviewer` — Non-binding; can mark reviewed but does not block approval progression.

The role, combined with the participant's current decision, also drives the `userInfo.action` value on every approval response — see [`Approval.userInfo.action`](#section/Schemas/Approval) for the role/decision → action mapping.

emailAddr.description: User's email address. Populated only when `participantType` is `USER`.

licenseType.description: Workfront license tier of the user. Populated only when `participantType` is `USER`.

eauthUserID.description: IMS-bound external authentication user ID. Populated only when `participantType` is `USER`.

avatarDate.description: Avatar version timestamp used to bust the avatar image cache. Populated only when `participantType` is `USER`.

teamMembers.description: Members of the team. Populated only when `participantType` is `TEAMOB`.

TemplateBody.description: Request body for creating or updating a template.

assetType.description: Asset type the template applies to.

stages.description: Stages that make up the template, in graph form.

stageId.description: Identifier for this template stage. Must be unique across all stages within the template (e.g. `design-review`).

parentIds.description: IDs of stages that must complete before this stage activates.

triggers.description: Activation triggers for the stage. Each entry defines when and how the stage becomes active relative to other stages on the template.

deadlineBusinessDays.description: Number of business days from stage activation to its deadline.

deadlineTimeOfDay.description:
Time-of-day component of the stage deadline. An object with two numeric properties — `hours` (0–23) and `minutes` (0–59) — that, paired with the date derived from `deadlineBusinessDays`, pins the deadline to a specific moment. Set to `null` when no time-of-day is configured.

Example: `{ "hours": 17, "minutes": 0 }` for 5:00 PM.

participantType.description:
Type of participant.

- `USER` — Individual Workfront user (or AI bot user).
- `TEAMOB` — Workfront team.

participantRole.description:
Role this participant plays on the stage.

- `approver` — Has a binding vote; their decision directly affects stage and approval status.
- `reviewer` — Non-binding; can mark reviewed but does not block approval progression.

sharedWith.description: Optional list of users the template is shared with.

errors.description: Validation error details (present on 400 FST_ERR_VALIDATION errors)

response.description: Upstream service error response, if applicable

guidelineAttribute.description: Attribute of the guideline being evaluated.

isBot.description: Whether this user is an AI bot participant.

participantIdType.description:
How `participantId` is typed:
- `wf_guid` — non-ESM assets.
- `ims` — ESM assets with an IMS `eauthUserID`.
- `email` — ESM assets without an IMS `eauthUserID`.

wfUserId.description: The Workfront internal user ID (32-character hex).

emailAddr.description: User's email address. Omitted when not available from Workfront.

isBot.description: Whether this team is an AI bot participant.

ParticipantMetadata.description:
Free-form metadata about the participant. The shape varies by `participantType`:

**`USER` participants**
```
{
  "isBot": boolean,
  "participantIdType": "wf_guid" | "ims" | "email",
  "wfUserId": "string",
  "emailAddr": "string"  // omitted when not available
}
```

**`TEAMOB` (team) participants**
```
{
  "isBot": boolean
}
```

See `ParticipantUserMetadata` and `ParticipantTeamMetadata` for the typed shapes. Additional context-specific properties may be present; consumers should treat unknown keys as informational.

TeamMember.description: Workfront user that is a member of a team participant.

ParticipantUserWfMetadata.description: Workfront-derived enrichment fields populated when `participantType` is `USER`.

avatarDate.description: Avatar version timestamp used to bust avatar cache.

ParticipantTeamWfMetadata.description: Workfront-derived enrichment fields populated when `participantType` is `TEAMOB`.

ParticipantWfMetadata.description: Workfront-derived enrichment fields for the participant. The shape depends on `participantType` — see `ParticipantUserWfMetadata` and `ParticipantTeamWfMetadata`.

ApprovalDeletedResponse.description: Returned when removing the last remaining participant on the approval causes the approval itself to be deleted. The body contains user-context flags only (no approval payload), with `deleted: true` to signal the cascading delete.

deleted.description: Always `true` on this response shape; the approval no longer exists.

isApprovalActive.description: Whether the version being accessed is the document's active head version.

totalGuidelineTraitsPassed.description: Number of guideline traits that passed.

totalCriticalGuidelineTraitsPassed.description: Number of critical guideline traits that passed.

totalNonCriticalGuidelineTraitsPassed.description: Number of non-critical guideline traits that passed.

validationSummary.description: Aggregate result across all checks for the asset (or page).

passed.description: Whether the asset passed validation overall.

imageGuidelines.description: Aggregate guideline-trait counts plus an overall pass flag and score.

totalGuidelineTraitsPassed.description: Number of guideline traits that passed.

totalCriticalGuidelineTraitsPassed.description: Number of critical guideline traits that passed.

totalNonCriticalGuidelineTraitsPassed.description: Number of non-critical guideline traits that passed.

fieldResults.description: Per-field validation results, keyed by field name.

botId.description: ID of the bot that produced this review.

brandId.description: ID of the brand associated with the bot.

categoryId.description: Category for this review, if any.

validationTypes.description: Validation types applied to this asset.

stageId.description: ID of the approval stage this review is attached to.

pdfDownloadUrl.description: Pre-signed URL to download a PDF of the review, when available.

bot.description: Display name of the bot at the time of review.

status.description: Lifecycle status of the review (e.g. completed, failed).

failureCode.description: Machine-readable failure code when the review could not complete.

multiPageValidationResult.description: Per-page validation results for multi-page assets, keyed by page identifier. Each value follows the same shape as `validationResult`.

ApprovalPathCreate.description: A path grouping stages together for a parallel or branching approval flow. Used in create requests.

pathId.description: Optional identifier for the path. Auto-generated if omitted.

stages.description: Stages belonging to this path. Each item follows the `StageBody` shape.

ApprovalPathUpdate.description: A path grouping stages together for a parallel or branching approval flow. Used in update (PUT) requests. `pathId` is required to identify the path being updated.

pathId.description: Identifier of the path to update

stages.description: Stages belonging to this path. Each item follows the `StageBody` shape.

ApprovalPath.description: A path in the approval response — a named group of stages that run in parallel with other paths.

structure.description: Stage dependency graph for this path

UnauthorizedResponse.description: Unauthorized — no valid auth credentials provided

BadRequestResponse.description: Bad Request — validation failure or invalid state

LengthRequiredResponse.description: Length Required — the service requires a `Content-Length` header. Send an empty JSON object (`{}`) as the request body.

get.description: Get approval details for an asset.

post.description: Creates an approval with the supplied stage data. The `stages` array in the body defines the approval structure and is used as-is. `templateId` is optional metadata: the caller is expected to have fetched the template separately (`GET /api/v1/templates/{templateId}`) and copied its stages into the request body. When `templateId` is supplied, it is recorded on the new approval and the corresponding template's `usageCount` is incremented and `lastUsedOnDate` is refreshed.

stages.description: Stage definitions used to build the approval. The server uses this array as-is — it does not look up or expand any referenced template. To create an approval from a template, the caller must fetch the template independently (`GET /api/v1/templates/{templateId}`), copy its `stages` into this array, and optionally pass the same `templateId` to record the source on the approval and increment its usage counter.

templateId.description: ID of the template used to create this approval, if any. Recorded on the approval for traceability and used to increment the template's `usageCount` and refresh `lastUsedOnDate`. The server does **not** load stages from the template — the caller must fetch the template via `GET /api/v1/templates/{templateId}` and copy its stages into the `stages` array.

paths.description: Parallel paths for the approval. Either `stages` or `paths` must be provided, not both. When `paths` is used, each path groups its own stages; `stages` should be omitted.

delete.description: Deletes the approval for the given asset. The asset must be in an editable state. Check `userInfo.isEditable` on the approval response to determine whether the requesting user can perform write operations.

204.description: Approval deleted successfully — no content returned

500.description: Internal Server Error. Also returned when the approval does not exist — the service returns `{"message":"Something went wrong","statusCode":500}` instead of 404 for a missing approval.

get.description: Returns suggested approval payloads derived from prior document versions, shaped for use when creating a new approval on the current asset. Results are ordered newest version first.

sourceVersion.description: Document version number this suggestion was derived from

post.description: Adds one or more stages to the approval for the given asset. If no approval exists for the asset yet, one is created automatically — so this endpoint can be used in place of `POST /api/v1/approvals/{assetType}/{assetId}` to create an approval and its initial stages in a single call. Returns 201 on success.

templateId.description: ID of the template used to create this approval, if any.

paths.description: Parallel paths to add. Either `stages` or `paths` must be provided.

put.description: Replaces all stages on an existing approval with the given stage data. The asset must be in an editable state. Check `userInfo.isEditable` on the approval response to determine whether the requesting user can perform write operations.

paths.description: Parallel paths to update. Either `stages` or `paths` must be provided.

patch.description: Partially updates any field on the specified stage. The asset must be in an editable state. Check `userInfo.isEditable` on the approval response to determine whether the requesting user can perform write operations.

delete.description: Deletes the specified stage from the approval. The asset must be in an editable state. Check `userInfo.isEditable` on the approval response to determine whether the requesting user can perform write operations.

500.description: Internal Server Error. Also returned when the approval does not exist — the service returns `{"message":"Something went wrong","statusCode":500}` instead of 404 for a missing approval.

put.description: Locks the specified stage. All decisions on the stage must have been made before locking. The asset must be in an editable state. Check `userInfo.isEditable` on the approval response to determine whether the requesting user can perform write operations.

requestBody.description: Send an empty JSON object. The service returns HTTP 411 if no body is provided.

put.description: Unlocks the specified stage, making it editable again. The asset must be in an editable state. Check `userInfo.isEditable` on the approval response to determine whether the requesting user can perform write operations.

requestBody.description: Send an empty JSON object. The service returns HTTP 411 if no body is provided.

post.description: Makes a decision on one or more stages. If the decision already exists it will be updated. Overall stage and approval status is recalculated after each decision. Throws an error if the approval is not found or the current user is not a participant.

decision.description: The decision value (e.g. `approved`, `needs work`, `approved with changes`, `reviewed`)

stageIds.description: Stage IDs to apply the decision to. Defaults to all active stages if omitted.

delete.description: Removes the current user's decision(s) from all active stages. The approval must be active.

500.description: Internal Server Error. Also returned when the approval does not exist — the service returns `{"message":"Something went wrong","statusCode":500}` instead of 404 for a missing approval.

post.description: Makes a decision on a specific stage. Equivalent to `POST /decisions` with a single stage ID.

delete.description: Removes the current user's decision from the specified stage.

500.description: Internal Server Error. Also returned when the approval does not exist — the service returns `{"message":"Something went wrong","statusCode":500}` instead of 404 for a missing approval.

get.description: Returns participant suggestions derived from the previous document version's approval.

participantRole.description:
Role this participant plays on the stage.

- `approver` — Has a binding vote; their decision directly affects stage and approval status.
- `reviewer` — Non-binding; can mark reviewed but does not block approval progression.

The role, combined with the participant's current decision, also drives the `userInfo.action` value on every approval response — see [`Approval.userInfo.action`](#section/Schemas/Approval) for the role/decision → action mapping.

prevVersionWithApprovers.description: The prior document version that had approvers

post.description: Adds one or more participants to an approval. If the approval does not yet exist, one will be created. If a participant already exists they will be merged and updated. The asset must be in an editable state. Check `userInfo.isEditable` on the approval response to determine whether the requesting user can perform write operations.

delete.description: Removes a participant from the approval. If all participants are removed, the stage and approval are also deleted. The asset must be in an editable state. Check `userInfo.isEditable` on the approval response to determine whether the requesting user can perform write operations.

200.description: OK. Returns the updated approval when participants remain. When removing this participant empties the approval, the approval is deleted and the response is an `ApprovalDeletedResponse` envelope with `deleted: true` instead of the full approval payload.

500.description: Internal Server Error. Also returned when the approval does not exist — the service returns `{"message":"Something went wrong","statusCode":500}` instead of 404 for a missing approval.

post.description: Sends a reminder notification to the specified participant. The approval must exist and be in an editable state. Check `userInfo.isEditable` on the approval response to determine whether the requesting user can perform write operations.

participantId.description: ID of the participant to remind. Must match the `participantId` in the URL path.

participantType.description:
Optional. Type of the participant being reminded.

- `USER` — Individual Workfront user (or AI bot user).
- `TEAMOB` — Workfront team.

participantRole.description:
Optional. Role of the participant being reminded.

- `approver` — Has a binding vote.
- `reviewer` — Non-binding.

post.description: Sends reminder notifications to all participants across all stages who have not yet made a decision.

requestBody.description: Send an empty JSON object at minimum. The service returns HTTP 411 if no body is provided.

post.description: Sends a reminder notification to a specific participant within a specific stage.

participantId.description: ID of the participant to remind. Must match the `participantId` in the URL path.

participantType.description:
Optional. Type of the participant being reminded.

- `USER` — Individual Workfront user (or AI bot user).
- `TEAMOB` — Workfront team.

participantRole.description:
Optional. Role of the participant being reminded.

- `approver` — Has a binding vote.
- `reviewer` — Non-binding.

post.summary: Remind undecided participants on a stage
post.description: Sends reminder notifications to all undecided participants within a specific stage.

requestBody.description: Send an empty JSON object at minimum. The service returns HTTP 411 if no body is provided.

get.description: Returns all approval templates visible to the current user.

post.description: Creates an approval template. A customer can create a maximum of 1000 templates. Requires template management permissions.

get.description: Returns a single approval template by ID.

500.description: Internal Server Error. Also returned when the template ID does not exist — the service currently returns 500 instead of 404 for unrecognized IDs.

put.description: Partially updates an approval template. Requires template management permissions.

patch.description: Partially updates an approval template. Requires template management permissions.

delete.description: Deletes a single approval template. Requires template management permissions.
Deletes multiple approval templates in one call. IDs are comma-separated in the path. Requires template management permissions.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of template IDs to delete

acknowledged.description: Whether MongoDB acknowledged the bulk-delete operation.

500.description: Internal Server Error. Also returned when none of the supplied IDs exist or are recognized — the service does not treat unrecognized IDs as a no-op in this case.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of Workfront bot users that are eligible to be added as reviewer participants on an approval. A bot reviewer is invoked automatically when its assigned stage becomes active.

id.description: The unique identifier for the bot

name.description: The name the creator gave to the bot

description.description: The description of what the bot does or is used for

isActive.description: Whether the bot has been enabled

brandId.description: The ID of the brand the bot is associated with

creatorId.description: The ID of the user who created the bot

customerId.description: The ID of the Workfront customer the bot is associated with

imsOrgId.description: The IMS organization ID associated with the bot

eauthUserID.description: The IMS user ID associated with the bot

wfUserId.description: The Workfront user ID associated with the bot

post.description: Returns AI brand-review results produced for a document version under an approval. Each review is generated when a bot reviewer participant runs against a stage on the approval and captures the bot’s pass/fail findings against the brand’s guidelines. Filter results by bot, document version, parent document, or stage to narrow the response to a specific asset, review run, or stage on its approval.

botEauthUserId.description: The Workfront eauthUserID of the bot. Maps to associatedTechnicalAccountId on the bot record.

parentObjId.description: ID of the parent object (the document).

objType.description: Type of the object being reviewed.

objId.description: ID of the object being reviewed (the document version).

stageId.description: ID of the stage on the approval.

post.summary: Bulk lookup approvals by asset ID
post.description: Simple bulk lookup of approvals for a known list of asset IDs within a specific asset type. Provide the asset type in the path and an array of `assetIds` in the body to retrieve their approvals in a single call. If no body is supplied, the endpoint returns approvals for projects owned by the requesting user that are currently in review.

assetIds.description: Asset IDs to look up approvals for.

assetType.description: Optional. Asset type in the body. The path-level `assetType` is authoritative.

requestBody.description: Send an empty JSON object at minimum. The service returns HTTP 411 if no body is provided.
